title: galium pumilum var. hirtellum
intro: Galium pumilum var. hirtellum, also known as short-beaked bedstraw, is a perennial herb belonging to the Rubiaceae family and is native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial agent. The health benefits associated with Galium pumilum var. hirtellum include its potential to support kidney function, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and respiratory infections. Galium pumilum var. hirtellum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health issues.
